sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Celta Vigo are in strong form, remaining unbeaten in their last 3 matches with notable wins like a 4-1 victory over Valencia and a gritty 1-0 win against Sevilla. Rayo Vallecano have shown mixed results, with a recent 3-1 win over Granada but heavy losses like 4-0 to Elche, indicating defensive vulnerabilities. Celta's consistency gives them an edge heading into this match.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head History
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Rayo Vallecano have managed to avoid defeat against Celta Vigo, with a 1-1 draw in September 2025 and a 2-1 victory in May 2025. Both matches saw goals from both sides, suggesting a tendency for competitive, open games. Rayo's slight historical advantage in recent head-to-heads could boost their confidence.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Celta Vigo, Borja Iglesias (7 goals this season) and Iago Aspas (key in past matchups with 2 goals against Rayo) will be crucial in attack. Rayo Vallecano will rely on Jorge de Frutos, who has 6 goals and was the player of the match in their last head-to-head draw. These players could decide the outcome.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Insights
sections.text: Celta Vigo rank 7th with 25 goals scored (6th in LaLiga) and a strong defensive record (4th fewest goals conceded at 20), showcasing balance. Rayo Vallecano, sitting 12th, struggle offensively with just 16 goals and have conceded 22, highlighting a gap in quality. Celta's superior stats and league position make them favorites.

sections.subtitle: Match Expectations
sections.text: Given Celta Vigo's better form, home advantage at Estadio Abanca Balaídos, and stronger season stats, they align with Sofascore's 48% home win prediction. Rayo Vallecano's defensive issues and inconsistent away form (recent 4-0 loss) suggest they may struggle. Expect a match with moderate goals and card activity based on recent trends.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Celta Vigo holds a narrow 1-0 lead over Rayo Vallecano at Estadio Abanca Balaídos. The home side took the lead through a well-taken goal by Sergio Carreira in the 40th minute, capitalizing on their attacking presence late in the half. Both teams have shown moments of promise in the first 45 minutes, with Celta Vigo slightly edging the game in terms of possession and shot creation. Rayo Vallecano, however, has not been far behind, creating opportunities of their own but failing to convert a big chance. The match remains finely poised as we head into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Celta Vigo has displayed a marginally more cohesive performance in the first half, maintaining a slight edge in ball possession and generating more shots on target. Their ability to enter the final third frequently and create a big chance that led to the goal showcases their intent to dominate at home. Defensively, they've held firm with several clearances and interceptions, though their aerial duels could use improvement. Rayo Vallecano, on the other hand, has matched Celta in final third entries and even surpassed them in penalty area entries, indicating a direct approach. Despite missing a big chance, their attacking setup looks threatening, and their physicality in duels, especially in the air, has been a strong point. However, they’ve lacked the finishing touch to level the score.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Celta Vigo, Sergio Carreira has been the standout performer, not only for his goal but also for his defensive contributions with clearances and interceptions. Hugo Álvarez has been pivotal in creating opportunities, providing the assist for the goal and showing creativity in the final third. On the Rayo Vallecano side, Carlos Martín has been a bright spark, involved in creating a big chance and showing energy in attack. Pedro Díaz has also contributed with key passes, attempting to unlock Celta’s defense. Both teams will rely on these players to make a difference in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating attacking spells between the two sides, with Celta Vigo starting strongly and maintaining significant pressure in the opening minutes. Their momentum peaked around the midway point of the half with consistent forward surges, culminating in the goal just before the break. Rayo Vallecano had their own dominant phases, particularly in a prolonged spell around the 15-minute mark, where they pushed hard for an equalizer. Their attacking intent waned slightly in the latter stages of the half, but they showed flashes of danger. The momentum shifts suggest both teams are capable of taking control in the second half if they capitalize on their opportunities.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Celta Vigo will likely aim to build on their lead by maintaining their attacking rhythm and tightening up defensively in aerial battles. They’ll want to exploit any gaps left by Rayo Vallecano’s forward pushes. Rayo, trailing by a single goal, are expected to come out with renewed vigor, pushing for an equalizer with their direct play and penalty area presence. The game could open up if Rayo commits more players forward, potentially leaving space for Celta to counter. Discipline will be key, as both teams have shown they can create chances, and a single moment of brilliance or lapse in concentration could decide the outcome.
